items.text:
Notice: Launch of CampusConnect App

Date: April 15, 9:00 AM
Location: Student Union, Room 210

The Office of Student Affairs is pleased to announce the launch of CampusConnect, a new mobile application designed to help students access services, view schedules, and receive real-time alerts about campus events. A demonstration session will be held in Room 210 on April 15 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, hosted by Ms. Patel from the Information Technology Office.

Key features include:

- Personalized class and exam reminders
- Direct messaging with academic advisors
- Live dining hall menu and shuttle tracking
- Secure access to digital ID and library account

A $5 fee will be charged once per academic year to cover server maintenance, automatically billed through the student portal. Students who attend the launch session will receive a complimentary launch month at no charge. Please bring a charged smartphone and your student ID. For questions, contact campusconnect@university.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, who will host the demonstration session?

options.A: A representative from the Office of Student Affairs
options.B: Ms. Patel from the Information Technology Office
options.C: An academic advisor from the registrar's office
options.D: A student volunteer from the user research team

questions.explanation: The notice states the demonstration session will be hosted by Ms. Patel from the Information Technology Office.

questions.stem: How is the annual maintenance fee collected?

options.A: Through automatic billing on the student portal
options.B: Through a check delivered to Student Affairs
options.C: Through monthly deductions from meal plans
options.D: Through a one-time cash payment at Room 210

questions.explanation: The notice specifies the $5 fee is automatically billed through the student portal.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who attend the launch session?

options.A: They are required to install the app immediately
options.B: They will be allowed to keep the app permanently
options.C: They will avoid paying the fee for that first month
options.D: They must volunteer to lead future training sessions

questions.explanation: The notice states attendees receive a complimentary launch month at no charge, implying they will not pay the fee for the first month.

items.text:
Notice: E-Waste Recycling Drive

Date: May 22-24, 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
Location: Parking Lot D, Engineering Quad

The Office of Sustainability will host a three-day electronic waste recycling drive to help students, faculty, and staff dispose of unwanted electronics safely. All collected items will be sorted by certified handlers and recycled in accordance with state regulations.

Accepted items:

- Laptops and tablets
- Mobile phones and chargers
- Printers and small appliances
- Cables, batteries, and computer mice

Not accepted:

- Large household appliances such as refrigerators
- Televisions over forty inches
- Items containing liquid or hazardous chemicals

Participants must remove personal data before drop-off; the office does not provide data wiping services. A free reusable tote will be given to the first 100 participants each day. Volunteers from the Engineering Sustainability Club will assist with sorting. Questions may be directed to ewaste@university.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, which item is NOT accepted at the drive?

options.A: A used smartphone with a charging cable
options.B: A forty-five-inch television from a dorm room
options.C: A laptop with a removable hard drive
options.D: A small kitchen appliance no longer in use

questions.explanation: The notice excludes televisions over forty inches; a forty-five-inch television would not be accepted.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the notice?

options.A: To recruit volunteers for the Engineering Sustainability Club
options.B: To raise awareness of state recycling regulations
options.C: To remind staff to back up data before campus storage closes
options.D: To inform the community about an electronic recycling drive

questions.explanation: The notice's purpose is to announce the upcoming three-day e-waste drive and explain how to participate.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about participants who arrive late on the third day?

options.A: They may be required to schedule a separate appointment
options.B: They will be turned away if they bring large appliances
options.C: They may not receive a free reusable tote on arrival
options.D: They must complete data wiping at the registration table

questions.explanation: Free totes go to the first 100 participants each day, so late arrivals on the third day may not receive one.
